title: clarkia gracilis subsp. albicaulis
intro: Clarkia gracilis subsp. albicaulis, also known as white-stemmed clarkia or mountain clarkia, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the evening primrose family that is native to the western United States and Canada. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as colds and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Clarkia gracilis subsp. albicaulis include relieving coughs, reducing congestion, and soothing sore throats, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory ailments. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and experience its health benefits.
